title: Chinese Cabbage Stir-Fry

ingredients.name: cabbage (napa or regular white cabbage), roughly shredded

subs.name: bok choy, baby spinach or sliced pointed cabbage (bandha gobi)
subs.notes: bok choy needs only 2 minutes; spinach needs only 1 minute

subs.name: 2–3 Kashmiri dried chillies for milder heat
subs.notes: Kashmiri chillies give a beautiful red-orange colour

subs.name: 1 tsp salt + a squeeze of lemon
subs.notes: soy adds depth and colour that salt cannot replicate

ingredients.name: rice or white vinegar
visual_checks: adds brightness; skip if you prefer a purer flavour
ingredients.notes: adds brightness; skip if you prefer a purer flavour

steps.text: Shred the cabbage into roughly 3 cm wide strips. Wash and spin or shake dry — excess water will cause the cabbage to steam rather than stir-fry.
Heat oil in a wok or wide kadai over the highest flame you have until it just begins to smoke. Add the dried chillies and stir for 10 seconds until darkened and fragrant.
Add garlic and ginger. Stir for 20 seconds — the garlic should be golden and fragrant but not burnt.

Tip in all the cabbage at once. Toss vigorously with the spatula, letting it sit on the heat for 30 seconds before tossing again. Continue this rhythm for 3–4 minutes until the cabbage is wilted with some crisp edges — it should retain texture, not turn mushy.
Add soy sauce and vinegar if using. Toss for 30 seconds until the seasoning is distributed evenly. Taste and adjust salt.
Remove from heat. Drizzle with sesame oil if using. Serve immediately as a side with rice and other dishes.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cabbage → bok choy, baby spinach or sliced pointed cabbage (bandha gobi) (bok choy needs only 2 minutes; spinach needs only 1 minute); soy-sauce → 1 tsp salt + a squeeze of lemon (soy adds depth and colour that salt cannot replicate); dried-red-chilli → 2–3 Kashmiri dried chillies for milder heat (Kashmiri chillies give a beautiful red-orange colour)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Bandha gobi (white cabbage) is the perfect substitute for napa cabbage — it is available year-round at every vegetable market in India. A kadai on high flame works as well as a wok. Kashmiri or guntur dried red chillies are ideal. This dish cooks in 7 minutes and goes with rice, roti or as a side alongside any dal. Gingelly oil (nallennai) finishes it beautifully.
